question_text: Which of the following is an example of a non-contact force?

options.label: A
options.text: Friction between a ball and the ground

options.label: B
options.text: The gravitational pull of the Earth on the Moon

options.label: C
options.text: A push from your hand on a door

options.label: D
options.text: Air resistance on a falling feather

explanation: Non-contact forces act at a distance without the objects touching. Gravity is a non-contact force that pulls objects together. Options A, C, and D are all contact forces that require objects to be touching or in direct contact with a medium.
